commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands Codex OAuth refresh-failure classification and guidance, removes legacy OAuth sidecar Keychain recovery, and updates OpenAI/Codex auth-profile routing and bootstrap logic with regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lacks several current Codex terminal refresh-token patterns and can choose openai-codex from stale auth.order.openai state, while the PR adds focused regression coverage. A live revoked-token session was not reproduced.
commands.review_followup: Draft protected MEMBER PR needs maintainer decision on the Keychain-only sidecar upgrade behavior before merge; automation should not choose that compatibility policy.

target.title: fix(auth): drop stale Codex OAuth routing
target.body:
## Summary

- expand OAuth refresh-failure classification to match current Codex refresh-token reuse, expiry, invalidation, revocation, and account-mismatch messages
- remove the legacy Codex OAuth sidecar/Keychain path and keep auth-profile persistence on the OpenClaw credentials boundary
- keep stale persisted/session-selected Codex auth profiles and stale `auth.order.openai` entries from switching OpenAI PI runs to `openai-codex`
- skip Codex harness auth bootstrap when there is no eligible forwarded plugin harness profile
- add changelog coverage for the Codex auth behavior and fold the #83345 fix into this PR

Fixes #83223.
Supersedes #83345.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.hints.test.ts src/commands/status-all/gateway.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`: passed, 12 files / 195 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`: passed, 3 files / 67 tests
- `git diff --check`: passed
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `check:changed`: passed on `tbx_01krwna67n49v7tsqfs4j59ke7`, Actions run `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26013454900`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server OAuth refresh failures now classify current Codex terminal refresh-token states precisely while user-facing messages explain that OpenClaw cannot use the user's OpenAI account for Codex until they sign in again. Generic account-state drift remains retryable/non-terminal. Stale Codex profile references from prior config/session state no longer force OpenAI PI runs onto the `openai-codex` provider when the requested route is `openai/gpt-5.5`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox wrapper using the OpenClaw CI check workflow.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -provider blacksmith-testbox -blacksmith-workflow ci-check-testbox.yml -blacksmith-job check -keep-on-failure --shell -- "git fetch origin main && pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused regression suites passed locally in the Codex worktree, and `check:changed` completed successfully on Testbox `tbx_01krwna67n49v7tsqfs4j59ke7` for commit `9bc371033cc`.

Observed result after fix: OAuth copy/classifier, stale profile boundary, Codex app-server auth bridge, doctor/status messaging, stale OpenAI PI routing, and harness bootstrap regression coverage all pass; the remote changed gate passes typecheck, lint, import-cycle, and changed-surface checks.

What was not tested: a live Docker session with a real revoked/expired OpenAI refresh token was not reproduced; coverage is source-level parity with current Codex messages plus mocked OAuth refresh paths and focused stale-routing regression tests.
target.author: vincentkoc
